articles.title: Hot Days, Wet Nights: Tamil Nadu to Battle Both Heatwave and Heavy Rain This Week - BizzBuzz
articles.full_text:
Hot Days, Wet Nights: Tamil Nadu to Battle Both Heatwave and Heavy Rain This Week
Tamil Nadu is facing an unusual weather week — scorching heat during the day and heavy rainfall in the evenings. The RMC has forecast rain across several districts including Theni, Madurai and Chennai for the next week. Here's the full district-wise forecast.
Hot Days, Wet Nights: Tamil Nadu to Battle Both Heatwave and Heavy Rain This Week
Tamil Nadu is caught between two extremes right now. Step outside during the day and the heat is brutal. But by evening, the skies in many parts of the state have been opening up with thunderstorms and gusty winds. Meteorologists say this unusual pattern is not going anywhere soon.
The Regional Meteorological Centre (RMC) has forecast rain across several districts of Tamil Nadu over the coming week, even as temperatures in the state continue to soar.
What's Causing This Weather Pattern?
The contrasting conditions — intense heat by day and rain by night — are being driven by shifts in the speed and direction of westerly winds. The IMD has forecast isolated to scattered rainfall over Tamil Nadu, Puducherry and Karaikal from June 15 to 20, along with thunderstorms, lightning and gusty winds reaching speeds of 40 to 50 kilometres per hour, gusting up to 60 kmph, between June 15 and 18.
Several districts in Tamil Nadu are likely to see rain along with thunderstorms and gusty winds, while Chennai is expected to stay partly cloudy with light rainfall predicted intermittently.
District-Wise Rain Forecast
Tuesday's forecast covers nine districts, including areas in the Western Ghats region along with Namakkal and Salem. Theni district has been placed under a heavy rain and thunderstorm alert. Moderate rainfall with gusty winds is expected in Namakkal, Salem, Tiruchy, Perambalur, Ariyalur, Kallakurichi, Thanjavur, Tiruvarur and Mayiladuthurai.
Over the next two days, heavy rainfall has been warned for Madurai, Ramanathapuram and Sivaganga districts as well. The RMC expects rainfall activity to persist across Tamil Nadu, Puducherry and Karaikal for at least another week.
Chennai Sizzles at 40°C
While rain provides some relief to interior and southern districts, the capital is sweating it out. Chennai's Meenambakkam and Nungambakkam weather stations both recorded a maximum temperature of 40 degrees Celsius on Monday — the highest in the state.
The forecast for the city shows partly cloudy skies with a chance of light showers in some pockets, but temperatures are unlikely to drop significantly.
Other locations that recorded severe heat on Monday include Madurai Airport, Madurai city and Tiruttani at 39°C, while Vellore, Nagapattinam, Thanjavur, Karur Paramathi, Tiruchy, Puducherry, Namakkal and Palayamkottai also baked under harsh conditions.
In all, twelve locations across the state recorded scorching temperatures on Monday, marking a sharp spike after relatively milder conditions in the weeks prior.
Rain Already Hitting Parts of the State
Despite the heat, Monday was not entirely dry. Showers hit Tambaram, Ariyalur and Senji, and heavy rain with strong winds lashed Melmalayanur, Valathi, Thayanur and Eeyakunnam for over an hour.
In the Chennai suburbs, rainfall was recorded in Pallavaram, Chromepet, Tambaram, Selaiyur, Mudichur and Vandalur. Ariyalur district also received light rain with thunder and lightning in areas like Royapuram, Senthurai, Vanjanapuram, Kudumur, Nallanayakapuram and Perumandi — giving residents a brief respite from the scorching heat.
What to Expect Going Forward
Meteorologists say the mixed bag of weather — fierce heat and scattered rain — is likely to continue across Tamil Nadu for the next several days.
People in the forecasted rain districts should stay cautious of gusty winds, lightning and possible waterlogging, while those in heatwave-prone areas should take precautions against heat stroke and stay hydrated, especially during midday hours.
